page: Amazon Web Services x8g.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, with a single thread per core. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the x8g.12xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities and exceptional memory subsystem performance. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test ranks in the top 25% with a score of 81,892.57, while the Geekbench multi-core score reaches 23,349.0, placing it in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is a key strength, with large-block read-only and write bandwidths reaching 368.85 GB/sec and 186.53 GB/sec respectively, both scoring in the top 10%. Database operations and static web serving throughput also achieve top-tier status, with the latter reaching 102.03 GB/sec. Single-core performance, such as the Geekbench single-core score of 1,764.0, remains in the average tier.

The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance is defined by its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and Arm-based architecture, which typically offers high resource density and power efficiency compared to traditional x86 architectures. However, workloads requiring local NVMe storage or hardware acceleration will face tradeoffs due to the complete absence of local disks and GPUs. Given the top-tier database operation benchmarks and high memory capacity, this server is suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services x8g.12xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized server instance powered by 48 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 768.0 GB of RAM, providing a high density of 16.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. Benchmark results highlight top-tier memory bandwidth, with large-block reads at 368.85 GB/sec and writes at 186.53 GB/sec, alongside top-tier database operations and static web serving throughput. Multi-core CPU performance is strong, though single-core metrics remain average. This hardware profile makes the instance highly efficient for memory-intensive applications, database management, and software compilation, though the lack of local storage represents a key architectural tradeoff.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8g.12xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized server with 48 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 768 GB RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory and database performance.

tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized instance delivering high memory bandwidth and dedicated core performance for database and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
